docs.title: Gear Tensioner Assembly
docs.text: View Materials List

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Build the front connector and gear train, then install lock and tensioning hardware.

docs.title: Step 1: Prepare inner gear screw and nut
docs.text:
• Insert a 1-1/4 inch 10-32 screw into InnerGear from the circular side.
  • Thread a 10-32 nut until seated in the underside cutout.
  • Apply a small amount of superglue to secure the screw head/threads and seat fully.
  • Allow adhesive to cure before continuing.

docs.title: Step 2: Build connector cap gear stack
docs.text:
• Place FrontConnectorCap on the selected shroud.
  • Fasten with two 6-32 x 5/16 screws in the inner bottom holes.
  • Lightly lubricate the gear bed and install BarrelGear, SideGears with 3/4 inch dowels, OuterGear, and InnerGear.
  • Place washer above InnerGear.

docs.title: Step 3: Prepare tensioner block
docs.text:
• Insert a 10-32 nut into TensionerBlock.
  • Press NutCap in until it clicks into side grooves.
  • Test fit the tensioner pin path across the block.

docs.title: Step 4: Install heat set insert
docs.text:
• Install a 6-32 heat set insert into FrontConnectorBase top hole.
  • Press straight and flush using a soldering iron at correct temperature.

docs.title: Step 5: Join base to cap assembly
docs.text:
• Align both dowels, the center inner-gear screw hole, and top side grooves.
  • Press FrontConnectorBase onto FrontConnectorCap until snug.

docs.title: Step 6: Install lock hardware
docs.text:
• Fasten selected shroud into FrontConnectorBase with one 6-32 x 5/16 screw from top.
  • Install two 6-32 x 5/8 socket head bolts from shroud side.
  • Insert two 6-32 nuts into side slots.
  • Install two LockBlocks (tapered side down), then LockBlockCap and secure with one 6-32 x 5/16 screw.

docs.title: Step 7: Install tensioner block and thumbscrews
docs.text:
• Insert TensionerBlock from bottom with smaller face up.
  • Thread two 6-32 thumbscrews into captive nuts above LockBlocks.
  • Rotate OuterGear to move TensionerBlock until through-hole aligns.
  • Verify thumbscrews drive LockBlocks inward, then back off for later barrel install.

docs.title: Components
docs.text:
• FrontConnectorCap
  • FrontConnectorBase
  • TensionerBlock
  • NutCap
  • LockBlock x2
  • LockBlockCap
  • BarrelGear
  • SideGear x2
  • OuterGear
  • InnerGear

docs.title: Hardware
docs.text:
• 3/32 inch Dowel, 3/4 inch x2
  • 10-32 Nut x2
  • 10-32 Oval Head Screw, 1-1/4 inch x1
  • 6-32 Heat Set Insert x1
  • Washer x1
  • 6-32 Nut x2
  • 6-32 Socket Head Bolt, 5/8 inch x2
  • 6-32 Phillips Screw, 5/16 inch x4
  • 6-32 Thumbscrew x2

docs.title: Consumables
docs.text:
• Superglue
  • Lubricant (light)

docs.title: Components
docs.text: • Railgun shroud or Bipod shroud (choose one)

docs.title: Step 1
docs.text: Prepare InnerGear with 10-32 screw and nut, then glue and cure.

docs.title: Step 2
docs.text: Build FrontConnectorCap gear stack and install washer.

docs.title: Step 3
docs.text: Install 10-32 nut and NutCap in TensionerBlock.

docs.title: Step 4
docs.text: Install 6-32 heat set insert in FrontConnectorBase.

docs.title: Step 5
docs.text: Align and press FrontConnectorBase onto FrontConnectorCap assembly.

docs.title: Step 6
docs.text: Install shroud screw, socket bolts, nuts, lock blocks, and LockBlockCap.

docs.title: Step 7
docs.text: Install TensionerBlock and thumbscrews, verify lock motion.

docs.title: Receiver Assembly
docs.text: View Materials List

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Assemble the trigger group into the receiver, install the grip and front locator dowel, and snap in the trigger guard.

docs.title: Step 1: Fit trigger and sear bushings
docs.text:
• Insert an 8 mm PTFE tube into the pivot hole of the Trigger.
  • Confirm a 3/32 inch dowel can pass through and rotate freely, then remove the dowel.
  • Repeat for the Sear.

docs.title: Step 2: Install trigger pivot
docs.text:
• Insert the Trigger into the bottom of the Receiver and align the holes.
  • Press a 3/32 inch dowel through the receiver and trigger until seated on both sides.
  • Verify the Trigger rotates freely.

docs.title: Step 3: Install sear and spring
docs.text:
• Move the Trigger forward and place a 0.625 inch compression spring in the receiver spring pocket.
  • Pull the Trigger back, position the Sear so teeth engage, and align pivot holes.
  • Press a 3/32 inch dowel through the sear pivot and release the Trigger to resting position.

docs.title: Step 4: Install grip
docs.text:
• Slide the Grip onto the back of the Receiver.
  • Secure with two 6-32 Phillips screws.

docs.title: Step 5: Install front locator dowel
docs.text:
• Install a 3/32 inch dowel in the top front slot of the Receiver.
  • Press fully into place.
  • If needed, lightly open the slot and use a small drop of superglue only if friction fit is insufficient.
  • ReceiverDualWindow variant: install the symmetric dowel on the opposite side.

docs.title: Step 6: Install trigger guard
docs.text:
• Hook the handle side of the TriggerGuard into the Receiver first.
  • Flex the front side in until it snaps into the front slot ahead of the Sear.

docs.title: Components
docs.text:
• Receiver
  • Trigger
  • Sear
  • Grip
  • TriggerGuard

docs.title: Hardware
docs.text:
• 6-32 Phillips Screw x2
  • 3/32 inch Dowel x3
  • 0.625 inch Compression Spring x1

docs.title: Consumables
docs.text: • Superglue (optional)

docs.title: Hardware
docs.text: • 3/32 inch Dowel x1 (additional)

docs.title: Step 1
docs.text: Fit PTFE tube bushings in Trigger and Sear.

docs.title: Step 2
docs.text: Install Trigger and pivot dowel in Receiver.

docs.title: Step 3
docs.text: Install compression spring, then install Sear and pivot dowel.

docs.title: Step 4
docs.text: Install Grip with two 6-32 Phillips screws.

docs.title: Step 5
docs.text: Install front locator dowel (plus second dowel on ReceiverDualWindow).

docs.title: Step 6
docs.text: Snap TriggerGuard into place.

docs.title: Shroud Assembly
docs.text: View Materials List

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Assemble one of the front shroud variants for your BABP build. This section covers both the standard railgun shroud and the railgun bipod shroud path.

docs.title: Variant Paths
docs.text:
• Railgun Variant
  • Bipod Sub-Assembly
  • Bipod Variant

docs.title: Step 1: Choose a Variant
docs.text: Select either the railgun variant or the railgun bipod variant before starting hardware installation.

docs.title: Step 2: Complete the Matching Variant Page
docs.text: Follow the linked variant page in full before continuing to Receiver Assembly.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Assemble the locking bipod mechanism before inserting it into the bipod shroud body.

docs.title: Step 1: Install Right Leg
docs.text: Insert RightLeg into the right side of BipodCore and secure with 1x 6-32 socket head screw. The indent should face inward.

docs.title: Step 2: Install Left Leg
docs.text: Insert LeftLeg into the left side of BipodCore and secure with 1x 6-32 socket head screw. Confirm both leg indents face inward.

docs.title: Step 3: Prepare Lock Nubs and Spring
docs.text: Optionally smooth the front angled sections of RightLockNub and LeftLockNub. Place both lock nubs in BipodCap with nub faces inward, then insert the spring between them.

docs.title: Step 4: Install Cap
docs.text: Install the cap assembly onto the bottom of BipodCore and secure with 1x 6-32 Phillips screw. Confirm lock nubs move freely and spring back.

docs.title: Step 5: Prepare Button Dowels
docs.text: Optionally smooth the inner center edges of BipodButton. Insert 2x 3/32 inch dowels into the left and right holes of the button.

docs.title: Step 6: Install Button
docs.text: Insert the button into the front of the core, align top guides, and confirm pressing the button retracts both lock nubs.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: This variant adds the integrated locking bipod and uses the bipod shroud body components.

docs.title: Step 1: Install Heat Set Insert
docs.text: Install 1x 6-32 heat set insert into RGB_Insert, flush to the part surface. Insert RGB_Insert into RGB_MainBody with the flat side up.

docs.title: Step 2: Insert Support Bars and Lock Insert
docs.text: Insert the longer RGB_TopSupportBar and shorter RGB_BottomSupportBar into the rear slots. Install 1x 6-32 Phillips screw into the bottom of RGB_MainBody to lock the insert and bottom support bar.

docs.title: Step 3: Attach Base
docs.text: Install RGB_Base to RGB_MainBody and secure with 5x 6-32 socket head screws. Do not overtighten.

docs.title: Step 4: Install Bipod Sub-Assembly
docs.text: Slide the completed Bipod Sub-Assembly into the front of RGB_MainBody. Use 1x 6-32 socket head screw through the button into the heat set insert. Keep it loose enough to allow swivel; Loctite is recommended.

docs.title: Step 5: Functional Check
docs.text: Confirm legs extend and lock, lock nubs engage in extended position, and bipod swivels side to side. Adjust screw tension if needed.

docs.title: Hardware
docs.text:
• 6-32 Socket Head Screw (see variant quantities)
  • 6-32 Phillips Screw (see variant quantities)

docs.title: Components
docs.text:
• RG_MainBody
  • RG_Base
  • RG_TopSupportBar
  • RG_BottomSupportBar
  • RG_BottomRail (chosen length)

docs.title: Hardware
docs.text:
• 6-32 Phillips Screw x3
  • 6-32 Socket Head Screw x5

docs.title: Components
docs.text:
• BipodCore
  • RightLeg
  • LeftLeg
  • RightLockNub
  • LeftLockNub
  • BipodCap
  • BipodButton

docs.title: Hardware
docs.text:
• 6-32 Socket Head Screw x2
  • 6-32 Phillips Screw x1
  • 3/32 inch Dowel x2
  • Bipod Lock Spring x1

docs.title: Components
docs.text:
• RGB_MainBody
  • RGB_Base
  • RGB_Insert
  • RGB_TopSupportBar
  • RGB_BottomSupportBar
  • Bipod Sub-Assembly

docs.title: Hardware
docs.text:
• 6-32 Heat Set Insert x1
  • 6-32 Phillips Screw x1
  • 6-32 Socket Head Screw x6

docs.title: Consumables
docs.text: • Loctite (recommended)

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: This variant uses the RG shroud body and support bars without the integrated bipod assembly.

docs.title: Step 1: Install Bottom Rail
docs.text: Install your chosen length of RG_BottomRail to RG_MainBody with 3x 6-32 Phillips screws.

docs.title: Step 2: Insert Support Bars
docs.text: Insert the longer RG_TopSupportBar and shorter RG_BottomSupportBar into the rear slots of RG_MainBody.

docs.title: Step 3: Attach Base
docs.text: Install RG_Base to RG_MainBody and secure with 5x 6-32 socket head screws. Do not overtighten.

docs.title: Step 1: Choose Variant
docs.text: Use the standard railgun or railgun bipod variant path.

docs.title: Step 2: Build Shroud Variant
docs.text: Complete either Railgun Variant or Bipod Variant.

docs.title: Step 3: Continue To Receiver
docs.text: After shroud variant completion, proceed to Receiver Assembly.
